perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) A young woman with long brown hair and red lipstick looks skeptical and confused while looking at a small video overlay showing five animated characters performing a synchronized dance. (2) The scene cuts to her in a bathroom setting, using a black hairdryer and smiling brightly while dancing to the rhythm. (3) She is seen in a subway station, holding a smartphone and swaying slightly. (4) In an airport setting, she holds a Dunkin' coffee cup and a paper bag, dancing enthusiastically near a seating area. (5) She shows a South Korean passport to the camera with a playful smile. (6) She continues to dance in the airport lounge while a female friend sitting next to her looks at her with an awkward, judging facial expression.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) An upbeat, high-tempo pop song titled "Soda Pop" begins immediately with clear female vocals singing lyrics about "soda pop." (2) The music remains continuous and consistent in energy level throughout the entire video. (3) There are no audible ambient noises from the subway or airport, nor are there any additional sound effects; the studio-recorded music track dominates the entire audio experience.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) "POV Saja Boys 什麼鬼? 🤨" - Hook text in Traditional Chinese expressing skepticism about a group. (2) "Don't Get the Hype: Saja Boys?" - Functional English subtitle for the hook. (3) "Soda Pop?" - Decorative text highlighting the song title. (4) "現實：Reality" - Functional text marking the transition to real-life behavior. (5) "(💬你才是鬼啊)" - Humorous decorative text acting as an inner monologue or friend's comment meaning "You're the weird one."

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro expressing skepticism -> Montage showing obsessed behavior (Reality).

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator appearing throughout, 1 female friend appearing briefly at the end.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian woman with long wavy brown hair, wearing a beige long-sleeved shirt with black text.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The video moves through various locations: a domestic room/bathroom, a modern subway platform, an airport food court with Dunkin' branding, and an airport departure lounge.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video is intended to be a humorous and relatable 'confession' about becoming obsessed with a trend or song one initially dismissed. By contrasting her initial skepticism towards the 'Saja Boys' with a montage of herself constantly dancing to their song in every aspect of her life, the creator uses self-deprecating humor to connect with viewers who have experienced the same 'earworm' effect.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The branding for Dunkin' and the South Korean passport appear as natural props in a travel vlog context rather than a paid promotion.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Usage of high-contrast text overlays and a mix of Traditional Chinese and English common in Taiwanese/Hong Kong social media content.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video effectively utilizes a popular audio trend to drive engagement through a relatable 'addiction to a song' narrative.
